Items.Title: 使用 2 台显示器进行学习和工作 | Use 2 monitors for learning and work
Items.Description:
在封面图片上，我正在第一台显示器上观看 youtube 教程，同时在第二台显示器上编写代码。

自从 3 年前我买了一个额外的显示器插入我的笔记本电脑，我无法想象没有它我自己会做任何严肃的事情。

作为一名 JavaScript 全栈开发人员，我总是学习新事物（前端、后端、移动、桌面、Chrome 扩展、报废、云部署等）

无论是有偿工作还是副业，工作流程大致相同。 为了感觉舒服，我需要同时在全屏 2 个应用程序中保持打开状态。

用例

  1. 我观看视频教程以了解有关新主题的基本想法，并在导师之后重复所有步骤

    • 第一台显示器 – youtube 视频
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

  2. 我阅读文档/书籍并在思维导图中做笔记，以深入理解材料。

    • 第一台显示器 – 文档站点
    • 第二台显示器——思维导图编辑器

  3. 我编码

    • 第一个监视器 - 编码结果（网页或移动应用程序模拟器）
    • 第二个监视器 - 代码编辑器

当您只有一台显示器时，您应该：

  • 在全屏窗口/应用程序之间切换或
  • 应用程序之间的分屏

在这两种情况下，闪烁的全屏应用程序或管理部分屏幕打开的应用程序的宽度不足会占用您大量的认知能量，并且您会更快地感到疲倦。 此外，您可能会伤害您的眼睛。

所以两个显示器是解决方案。 同时打开的两个全屏应用程序让您在管理信息、吸收新知识和轻松完成工作方面拥有巨大优势。

例如，这就是我使用 2 个监视器对 NextJS 文档进行思维导图的方式：

硬件设置

如果你在谷歌上搜索“多显示器”之类的东西——然后查看图像，你就会对它们的设置产生错误的认识，因为很多图像都是关于水平设置的，这会在一天之内扼杀你的脖子。 相反，您应该选择垂直设置——这样您就可以将眼睛从一台显示器切换到另一台显示器，而不会拉伤脖子。

你不需要一个棘手的小工具架——任何大小合适的罐子或盒子都是合适的。 我多年来一直使用宜家的 Pyssla 罐子作为显示器的支架，它非常好：

操作系统设置

就像谷歌搜索一样，操作系统会将您推向水平设置。 但是，您可以更改桌面设置中的位置。 当操作系统设置跟随现实生活中显示器的位置时很好，这样您就可以直观地工作。

没钱买吗？

即使你是穷人：学生、转行、初入职场、缺钱，我强烈建议你尽快购买便宜的二手显示器，因为它有时会提高你的效率。

也许你已经注意到我的第一张照片：我的显示器很旧，有两条彩色垂直线的缺陷。 我的笔记本电脑很旧，没有钥匙“；/：”。 所以我什至羞于向你展示它们。 但它们一起是学习和工作的酷工具。 我更喜欢这样的一对，而不是我拥有的 mackbook。



原文:

On the cover image I'm watching youtube tutorial on 1st monitor and simultaneously write code in 2nd monitor.

Since I bought an extra monitor to plug into my laptop 3 years ago, I can’t imagine myself doing anything serious without it.

As a JavaScript full-stack developer I always learn new things (Frontend, Backend, Mobile, Desktop, Chrome extensions, Scrapping, Cloud deployment etc.)

Whether it’s a paid job or a side project, the working process is generally the same. And to feel comfortable I need to keep opened in full screen 2 apps at the same time.

Use cases

  1. I watch a video tutorial to get basic ideas about the new subject, and repeat all steps after the tutor

    • 1st monitor – youtube video
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

  2. I read documentation/book and take notes in mindmap for deep understanding of material.

    • 1st monitor – documentation site
    • 2nd monitor – mindmap editor

  3. I code

    • 1st monitor – result of coding (web page or mobile app emulator)
    • 2nd monitor – code editor

When you have only 1 monitor, you should either:

  • switch between full-screen windows/apps or
  • split screen between apps

In both cases, blinking full screen apps or managing lack of width in part screen opened apps will take a lot of your cognitive energy, and you get tired much faster. Also, you can harm your eyes.

So two monitors is the solution. Simultaneously opened, two full-screen apps give you a big advantage to manage information, absorb new knowledge and do your work with fewer efforts.

Example, that's how I mindmaped NextJS docs using 2 monitors:

Hardware setup

If you google something like “multiple monitors” – and look at images, you get the wrong idea about their setup, because lots of images are about Horizontal setup, which will kill your neck in one day. Instead, you should choose a vertical setup – so you can switch your eyes from one monitor to another without straining your neck.

You don’t need for that a tricky gadget holder – any jar or box with proper size is suitable. I use an Ikea's Pyssla jar as a stand for monitor for years, and it is pretty good:

Operational System setup

Just as google search, OS will push you to horizontal setup. However, you can change positioning in desktop settings. That relative position of 1 to 2 affects on how mouse cursor will be moving between monitors. It is good when OS settings follow along with real life monitors’ position, so you will work intuitively.

Have not money for it?

Even if you are a poor: student, career switcher, beginner in job, with lack of money, I highly recommend you to buy even a cheap second hand monitor as soon as possible, because it will increase your efficiency at times.

Maybe you have noticed on the first picture with me: my monitor is old, with defect of two colored vertical lines. And my laptop is old without a key ";/:". So I even ashamed to show them to you. But together they are a cool tool for study and work. I prefer such couple over mackbook that I have.
Items.Author: apayrus

Items.Title: 使用一台显示器作为程序员的完整指南。 | The Complete Guide To Using One Monitor As A Programmer.
Items.Description:
关于一台显示器是否优于多台显示器的争论已经持续多年。 在决定哪种显示器设置最适合您时，需要考虑许多因素，例如您的工作环境、显示器的尺寸和类型，以及您每天使用的应用程序数量。

无论您的需求是什么，总有一款适合您的显示器设置。 显示器有不同的尺寸、形状和分辨率。 最常见的是 30 英寸对角线尺寸。 这是大多数人的标准，并提供标准的观看体验。 多台显示器允许您同时在两个屏幕上展开工作，许多人认为这可以提供更好的整体工作环境，因为它可以帮助您专注于您正在做的事情。 但是，这是个人喜好问题，通常取决于您正在执行的任务。

在本文中，我们将讨论在日常工作中使用一台显示器的一些主要好处，以及有效利用显示器的键盘快捷键。 如果您是 Linux 用户，那么这篇文章绝对适合您，尤其是如果您使用的是 Ubuntu。 如果不是这种情况，请尝试寻找一些替代方案。

使用一台显示器的好处

一个常见的误解是一台显示器不足以进行编码。 实际上，它可能非常有效。
虽然，很多人多年来一直在使用多台显示器。 但是，如果您是真正编码的开发人员，那么仅使用一台显示器进行编码和开发会有一些很大的好处。

第一个好处是您可以节省桌面空间。 使用一台显示器，您不必担心每次需要编码或处理其他事情时清理办公桌。 这将使您的办公桌远离杂乱和干扰，以便您更轻松地专注于工作。

第二个好处是，如果屏幕分辨率足够高，您将更容易看到眼前的代码，而无需在两个屏幕之间来回滚动。

第三个好处是你的大脑将能够专注于手头的任务，因为屏幕不会分成两部分。 这意味着您的工作将更易于跟踪，并且您在工作时会更加清晰。我将拥有一台大显示器，但我可能会使用第二台显示器来做其他事情（例如浏览器） ）。

第四个好处是您将能够更好地执行多项任务，因为屏幕的“占地面积”将更小。 这意味着您的显示器占用的空间会更少，因此您不会在一个屏幕上看到两个或三个窗口。 这样可以更轻松地运行多个应用程序，而无需不断关闭它们。 例如，您可以打开一个电子表格和一个浏览器，并且可以在它们之间快速移动。

最后一个好处是屏幕更容易阅读，因为它的边框更小。 这意味着当您阅读屏幕上的内容时，您的眼睛不需要在屏幕上移动那么远，这将使他们感觉更舒适。

使用一台显示器的隐藏功能

使用一台显示器而不是多台显示器的主要优点之一是它迫使您一次专注于一项任务。

研究表明，多任务处理实际上会降低生产力和效率[0]。 它还可能导致更多的压力甚至身体疼痛[1]。 使用单个监视器可以降低发生这种情况的风险。 这是主要好处之一。 尝试同时查看两个屏幕时，它还可以减轻眼睛的压力，减少眼睛疲劳和头痛。

使用一台显示器的好处很多。 一台显示器更便宜、更方便，并且对于多任务处理更有用。 此外，在单个屏幕上与他人分享您正在做的事情比在多个屏幕上更容易。 在同一屏幕上比较两个对象也更容易。

多台显示器的主要缺点之一是它们通常比单台显示器更难移动和设置，即使经过专家调整也是如此。 另一个缺点是每个屏幕都必须由专家调整到正确的位置和尺寸，这比调整单个显示器更困难。

在下一节中，我将分享一些我每天使用的快捷方式列表，这些快捷方式可以帮助我在应用程序等之间轻松导航。

包起来

这构成了我们在日常工作中使用单个显示器的指南。 我们已经讨论了使用单个显示器的优点以及使用多个显示器的缺点。 我们还介绍了可帮助您轻松管理环境的键盘快捷键列表。

与往常一样，本文是送给您的礼物，您可以与任何您喜欢的人分享或以任何有利于您的个人和职业发展的方式使用它。 通过支持这个博客，你让我有动力发布与 python 相关的高质量内容。 预先感谢您的最终支持！

编码快乐，伙计们； 下一篇见。

参考

多任务的多重成本。 大脑。 2019;2019:cer-04-19. 2019;2019:cer-04-19。

[2] Ubuntu, Windows and workspaces . [2] Ubuntu、 Windows 和工作区。



原文:

The debate over whether one monitor is better than multiple monitors has been going on for years. There are many factors to consider when deciding which monitor setup is best for you, such as your work environment, the size and type of your display, and the number of applications you use on a daily basis.

No matter what your needs are, there is a monitor setup that will be perfect for you. Monitors come in different sizes, shapes, and resolutions. The most common one is the 30-inch diagonal size. It’s the standard for most people and provides a standard viewing experience. Multiple monitors allow you to spread out your work on two screens simultaneously, which many argue can provide a better working environment overall because it can help you to focus on what you’re doing. However, this is a matter of personal preference and often depends on the tasks you are performing.

In this article, we are going to discuss some of the key benefits of using one monitor on your daily job, along with keyboard shortcuts to efficiently utilize your monitor. If you are a Linux user, then this article is definitely for you, especially if you are using Ubuntu. If it is not the case, then try to find some alternatives.

👉 Table Of Content (TOC).

Benefits Of Using One Monitor

🔝 Go To TOC.

It is a common misconception that one monitor is not enough for coding. In reality, it can be very efficient.
Although, a lot of people have been using multiple monitors for years now. However, if you're a developer who really codes, there are some great benefits to using just one monitor for coding and development.

The first benefit is that you'll be able to save on desk space. With one monitor, you won't have to worry about clearing off your desk every time you need to code or work on something else. This will allow you to keep your desk clear of clutter and distractions so that it's easier for you to focus on your work.

The second benefit is that if the screen resolution is high enough, it will be easier for you to see the code in front of you without having to scroll back and forth between the two screens.

The third benefit is that your brain will be able to focus on the task at hand because the screen won't be divided into two parts. This means that your work will be easier to track and you'll have more clarity when you're working.I'm going to have one large monitor, but I might use a second monitor for something else (like a browser, for example).

The fourth benefit is that you'll be able to multitask better, because the screen will have a smaller "footprint." This means that the amount of space your monitor takes up will be less, and therefore you won't see two or three windows on one screen. This makes it easier to run multiple applications without constantly closing them. For example, you can have a spreadsheet open and a browser open, and you'll be able to move between them quickly.

The final benefit is that the screen will be easier to read because it'll have smaller borders. This means that your eyes won't need to travel as far across the screen when they're reading something on it, which will make it more comfortable for them.

Hidden Features of Using One Monitor

🔝 Go To TOC.

One of the main advantages of using one monitor instead of multiple monitors is that it forces you to focus on one task at a time.

Studies show that multitasking can actually reduce productivity and efficiency[0]. It can also lead to more stress and even physical pain[1]. Using a single monitor reduces the risk of this happening. This is one of the main benefits. It also puts less strain on your eyes when trying to view two screens at once, reducing eyestrain, headaches.

The benefits of using one monitor are numerous. One monitor is cheaper, more convenient, and more useful for multitasking. In addition, it's easier to share what you're doing with others on a single screen than on multiple screens. It's also easier to compare two objects on the same screen.

One of the major disadvantages of multiple monitors is that they are generally more difficult to move and set up than a single monitor, even after being adjusted by an expert. Another disadvantage though is that each screen must be adjusted for the correct position and size by an expert, which is more difficult than adjusting a single monitor.

In the next section, I am going to share a handful list of shortcuts i use on a daily basis that help me to easily navigate between applications and such.

Linux Keyboard Shortcuts

🔝 Go To TOC.
  • 🪟 + ↑: Maximize the app window.

  • 🪟 + →: Move the app window to the right half of the screen.

  • 🪟 + ↓: Minimize/Detach the app window.

  • 🪟 + ←: Move the app window to the left half of the screen.

  • ctrl + alt + ↑: Move to the previous workspace.

  • ctrl + alt + ↓: Move to the next workspace.

  • alt + tab: Move between applications.

Wrapping Up

🔝 Go To TOC.

This constitutes our guide on using a single monitor for your daily work. We have discussed the pros of using a single monitor, and the cons of multiple monitors. We also went over a list of keyboard shortcuts that help you easily manage your environment.

As always, this article is a gift to you, and you can share it with whomever you like or use it in any way that would be beneficial to your personal and professional development. By supporting this blog, you keep me motivated to publish high-quality content related to python. Thank you in advance for your ultimate support!

Happy coding, folks; see you in the next one.

Reference

🔝 Go To TOC.

[0] American Psychological Association, Multitasking undermines our efficiency, study suggests.

[1] Madore KP, Wagner AD. Multicosts of multitasking. Cerebrum. 2019;2019:cer-04-19.

[2] Ubuntu, Windows and workspaces.
Items.Author: wiseai

Items.Title: 在 Expedia 的 2 年教会了我什么 | What 2 years at Expedia Taught Me
Items.Description:
嘿！ 有一阵子了。 事实上，距离我上一篇博文已有一年多的时间了。 从那以后，发生了很多事情，我有很多时间来反思这一切！ 这是另一天的另一篇文章，但今天我想谈谈我在 Expedia Group 工作的经历以及我学到的一些教训。

我于 2017 年秋天在密歇根州安娜堡加入了 Expedia。他们的办公室（包括我自己在内的许多人都不知道存在）最初是一家名为Mobiata的年轻创业公司，该公司很早就被收购了。 这就是我们亲切地称呼它的 Expedia A2 的由来。 我非常喜欢在这里工作； 这确实是一份梦寐以求的工作。 并不是说我对旅​​行一无所知，而是与我一起工作的每个人都很关心。 那时我不知道，但从团队环境到我们构建的产品，它产生了天壤之别。

得到教训

面试

与我当时正在经历的其他面试相比，面试过程非常令人耳目一新。 在最初的电话采访中，我出现了焦虑症，导致了极度的脑雾。 我被问及如何在guard声明中快速展开选项并完全空白，具有讽刺意味的是，这是我最喜欢的。 面试官理解并帮助引导我找到答案，这让我能够强调我是如何使用该语句来清理我最近正在处理的代码库中的嵌套 if 语句的。 其他采访会继续进行并将我注销。 最后一个阶段是半天的面对面采访，与团队、经理会面，然后进行一轮白板、现场编码会议和团队午餐。 时间很长，但它教会了我关于面试的最有力的一课，从那以后我应用于每个角色。 一路走来，我从不孤单。 采访我的人都和我一起解决了这个问题。 如果我被卡住了，我可以提出问题或澄清。 如果我想要他们的想法，我只是问。 每一步都明确了期望。 这是一个配对练习； 我们想知道您如何与我们合作，而不仅仅是您一个人。 是的，需要解决问题，但这次面试是我经历的第一个反映团队环境的过程。

一个团体

我们所做的一切都是作为一个团队。 我选择强调这个口头禅，因为它引起了我的共鸣，并且在领导链上很明显。 我不仅感觉与我的领导和他的经理有联系，而且我们的福利很棒，我们的假期很棒，而且无论他们的一名员工生活如何，他们似乎都支持你。

其中一项福利是志愿者日，办公室内的任何人都可以参加当地的志愿者活动以回馈社区。 一年我们在当地公园清理入侵物种，接下来是为有需要的人分类农产品。 这种前瞻性方法令人耳目一新，而不是大多数公司提供的典型的不干涉货币匹配。

当您的团队和您的公司为您的健康、幸福和社区投资时，每天上班和全力以赴成为一个简单的选择。 错误发生

虽然我真的很喜欢我在 Expedia 的时光，但没有什么是完美的。 有一个“假设积极意图”的价值。 虽然这对我来说是新的，但它绝对有意义。 我在这种模式下工作得越多，与其他人谈论他们的生活经历，我就越意识到它弊大于利。 虽然我会很快再次与我的许多同事一起工作，但很明显，有些人会依靠这个价值来帮助他们摆脱错误的选择，无论是否有意识。 行为模式清楚地表明，这是他们留在公司的生存方式。 作为实践中这种危害的一个例子，移动领域的一位突出领导者习惯于 my-way 或 high-way。 他们会倾听，但也如此专注于他们的思考过程，以至于从来没有真正感觉到相反的想法被考虑在内。 在更大的团队内部进行了对话，以便在您的更改上线之前进行更改，这绝不是一个好兆头。 我在这里最大的教训是，虽然看起来某事或某人有良好的意图，但要寻找模式。 作为一家企业，不断获得有关您的实践和价值观的反馈。 当有人说他们正因某件事而受到伤害时，请倾听，然后采取行动加以改善。

保持谦虚

为 Expedia 工作是令人兴奋的，并且足以让我们涉足“大型科技”领域，这仍然是一次很好的体验。 我真的很喜欢我们所做的工作、我们构建的产品以及我们培养的团队环境。 没想到一眨眼就变了。

在 COVID-19 撕裂旅游业之前，Expedia 在 2020 年初经历了大规模裁员。 虽然许多优秀的员工被解雇了，但我们整个下（小）办公室也是如此。 尽管我们是顶级制作团队之一，并且在领导链上获得了很好的评价，但我们并不是无敌的。 这对我们来说没有意义，但这只是一个数字游戏。 虽然我为失去一份出色的工作而感到悲痛，但在看到接下来的一年给世界带来的一切之后，我很感激它以它的方式发生。 没有这个，我就不会在下一份工作中获得高级职位。 如果没有这个，我就不会离开科技行业并与职业教练和人才顾问联系，看看我有什么未来。 如果没有这些偶然事件，我不会成为今天的我，领导一个移动团队并为他们提供我所享受的同样的支持和领导。



原文:

Hey there! It's been a while. As a matter of fact, over a year since my last blog post. Since then, a lot has happened, and I've had a lot of time to reflect on it all! That's another post for another day, but today I want to talk about my experience working at Expedia Group and some of the lessons I learned.

I joined Expedia in Ann Arbor, Mi, in the fall of 2017. Their office (which many, including myself, didn't know existed) began as a young startup called Mobiata that had been acquired early on. That's how Expedia A2, as we lovingly called it, came to be. I thoroughly enjoyed working here; it was indeed a dream job. Not that I'm head over heels about travel, but every person I worked with cared. I didn't know it then, but it made a world of difference from the team environment to the product we built.

Lessons Learned

Interviewing

The interview process was exceptionally refreshing compared to the other interviews I was going through at the time. During the initial phone interview, I had an anxiety attack resulting in extreme brain fog. I was asked about unwrapping optionals in swift and completely blanked on the guard statement, which was ironically my favorite. The interviewer understood and helped guide me to the answer, which allowed me to highlight how I've used the statement to clean up nested if statements in a codebase I was working on recently. Other interviews would have just moved on and written me off. The last phase was a half-day in-person interview to meet the team, the manager, then go through a round of whiteboarding, a live coding session and team lunch. It was long, but it taught me the most powerful lesson about interviewing that I've applied to every role since. Every step of the way, I was never alone. Whoever was interviewing me worked through the problem with me. If I was stuck, I could ask questions or clarification. If I wanted their thoughts, I just asked. Expectations were made clear every step of the way. This is a pairing exercise; we want to know how you work with us, not just on your own. Yes, problem-solving was needed, but this interview was the first process I went through that reflected the team environment.

One Team

"One Team" was a mantra used to drive home that you're not alone in success or failure. Everything we did was as a team. I chose to highlight this mantra because it resonated with me and was evident up the leadership chain. Not only did I feel connected to my leader and his manager, but our benefits were great, our leave was terrific, and no matter what life throws at one of their employees, it seemed like they had your back.

"One Team" also echoed out into the community. One of the perks was a volunteer day, where anyone within the office could attend a local volunteer event to give back to the community. One year we cleaned up invasive species in a local park, and the next was sorting produce for people in need. This forward approach was a refreshing take rather than the typical hands-off monetary match most corporations offer.

When your team and your company are invested in your health, happiness, and community, it makes going to work and putting your best foot forward every day an easy choice. It's been two years since Expedia A2 closed, and I still stay in touch with almost everyone.

Mistakes Happen

While I truly loved my time at Expedia, nothing is perfect. There was a value of "assume positive intent". While it was new to me, it made absolute sense. The more I worked under this model and talked with other people about their lived experiences, the more I realized it was doing more harm than good. While I'd work with many of my co-workers again in a heartbeat, it became evident that some would count on this value to bail them out of their bad choices, conscious or not. The pattern of behaviors made it clear that this was their survival method of staying with the company. As an example of this harm in practice, a prominent lead in the mobile space had a habit of my-way or the high-way. They would listen but were also so wrapped up in their thought process that it never really felt like the opposing thoughts were considered. There were conversations within the greater team to get your changes in before they came online, which is never a good sign. My biggest lesson here is while it may seem like something or someone has good intentions, look for the patterns. As a business, constantly get feedback on your practices and values. When someone says they are experiencing harm from something in place, listen, then act to improve it.

Stay Humble

Working for Expedia was exciting and enough of a toe-dip into the "Big Tech" scene that it was still a good experience. I truly loved the work we did, the product we built, and the team environment we fostered. I didn't consider that it could all change within a blink of an eye.

Expedia went through mass layoffs early in 2020 before COVID-19 tore apart the travel industry. While many excellent staff members were laid off, so was our entire off (small) office. Despite being one of the top-producing teams, and getting great reviews up the leadership chain, we weren't invincible. It didn't make sense to us, but it was just a numbers game. While I mourned the loss of a fantastic job, after seeing what the following year(s) gave the world, I'm thankful it happened the way it did. Without that, I wouldn't have gone for a senior title at my next job. Without that, I wouldn't have been one insincere review away from leaving tech and reaching out to a career coach and talent advisor to see what future I had. I wouldn't be where I am today without those happenstances, leading a mobile team and providing them with the same support and leadership I've enjoyed.
Items.Author: kaydacode

Items.Title: 使用 Caddy 进行安全 HTTP/3 实验 | Safe HTTP/3 Experimentation With Caddy
Items.Description:
我正在编写QUIC 文档，并认为将站点作为 HTTP/3（在 QUIC 之上运行）提供会很棒。 这是一个相对较新的协议，所以我遇到了一些熟悉的问题：

  • 工具：我使用的网络服务器 (apache) 不提供 HTTP/3 支持，并且目前没有计划
  • 正常运行时间：有问题的页面比较受欢迎，我不想在设置时冒险让它崩溃

幸运的是，QUIC 传输协议的细节给了我们一些灵活性：

  • 所有支持 HTTP/3 的浏览器都将尝试 QUIC，如果出现连接错误，将回退到 TCP（以及未修改的 apache 设置）

解决方案

修复是一个简单的 Caddy 反向代理。 它在 docker 容器中运行，让我“拆分绑定”：Caddy 想要绑定 TCP 和 UDP 端口，但我只能给它 UDP 端口。 容器化让我可以灵活地让它在它的容器中绑定，但只将 UDP 端口暴露给世界。

细节

您需要调整几行，用“ youruser ”或“ yoursite ”占位符表示：

设置：

 ### change as appropriate for your OS sudo snap install docker mkdir ~/caddy/ mkdir ~/caddy/caddy_data mkdir ~/caddy/caddy_config

启动：

 cd ~/caddy sudo docker-compose up -d ### tail the logs with `sudo docker logs -f caddy`

广告 HTTP/3

上面设置了一个反向代理，它在 UDP 端口 443 上提供 HTTP/3，但是在你在“真正的”HTTP 服务器上发布它之前，什么都不会尝试。 幸运的是，这个小配置是生产服务器上唯一需要的更改：

在我的网站的VirtualHost apache 配置中：

 Header set alt-svc "h3=\":443\"; ma=3600, h3-29=\":443\"; ma=3600

这会发布带有“Alt-Svc:”标头的 HTTP/3 服务（协议的“标准”和“草案 29”版本）。 您需要反弹 apache 才能使其生效。

证书轮换

最后一件事是处理证书轮换。 上述解决方案将您的站点证书和密钥复制到 Caddy 容器中，但如果您使用的是 LetsEncrypt，则该证书只能使用三个月，并且可能每月轮换一次。 我在 cron 中运行以下命令以确保容器始终捕获相对较新的证书：

 ### bounce the reverse proxy every month 39 0 25 * * cd /home/youruser/caddy && /path/to/docker-compose down && /path/to/docker-compose up -d

斯蒂芬·克利里CC 2.0 的封面图片



原文:

I'm writing QUIC documentation and thought it would be great to have the site available as HTTP/3 (which runs on top of QUIC). This is a relatively new protocol, so I ran into some familiar problems:

  • Tooling: The webserver that I'm using (apache) doesn't offer HTTP/3 support, and has no current plans to
  • Uptime: The page in question is relatively popular, and I don't want to risk it going down while I set this up

The QUIC Possibility

Luckily the details of the QUIC transport protocol give us some flexibility here:

  • QUIC uses a different underlying protocol (UDP vs TCP), so I can leave apache on TCP port 443 and put up a reverse proxy on the UDP port
  • All HTTP/3-capable browsers will try QUIC and, if they get a connection error, will fall back to TCP (and the unmodified apache setup)

The Solution

The fix is a simple Caddy reverse proxy. It's running in a docker container to let me "split the bindings": Caddy wants to bind both TCP and UDP ports but I can only give it the UDP port. Containerizing gives me the flexibility of letting it bind both in its container, but only exposing the UDP port to the world.

Details

The Caddy installation looks like this. You'll want to tweak a few lines, indicated with "youruser" or "yoursite" placeholders:

setup:

### change as appropriate for your OS
sudo snap install docker

mkdir ~/caddy/
mkdir ~/caddy/caddy_data
mkdir ~/caddy/caddy_config

~/caddy/docker-compose.yaml:

version: "3.7"

services:
  caddy:
    container_name: caddy
    hostname: caddy
    image: caddy:2.4.6
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "443:443/udp"
    volumes:
      - /home/youruser/caddy/Caddyfile:/etc/caddy/Caddyfile
      - /path/to/yoursite/fullchain.pem:/caddy.crt
      - /path/to/yoursite/privkey.pem:/caddy.key
      - /home/youruser/caddy/caddy_data:/data
      - /home/youruser/caddy/caddy_config:/config
    extra_hosts:
      - "host-gateway:172.17.0.1"

volumes:
  caddy_data:
    external: true
  caddy_config:

~/caddy/Caddyfile:

{
    auto_https off
    servers {
        protocol {
            experimental_http3
        }
    }
}

yoursite.com {
    tls /caddy.crt /caddy.key
    reverse_proxy * https://host-gateway {
        transport http {
            tls_insecure_skip_verify
        }
    }
}

startup:

cd ~/caddy
sudo docker-compose up -d
### tail the logs with `sudo docker logs -f caddy`

Advertising HTTP/3

The above sets up a reverse proxy that serves HTTP/3 on UDP port 443, but nothing will try it until you advertise it on your "real" HTTP server. Fortunately this minor config was the only change needed on the production server:

In my VirtualHost apache config for the site:

Header set alt-svc "h3=\":443\"; ma=3600, h3-29=\":443\"; ma=3600

This advertises an HTTP/3 service (both "standard" and "draft 29" versions of the protocol) with the "Alt-Svc:" header. You'll need to bounce apache for this to take effect.

Cert Rotation

One last thing is needed to handle cert rotation. The above solution copies your site certificate and key into the Caddy container, but if you're using LetsEncrypt that cert is only good for three months and is likely being rotated monthly. I run the following in cron to ensure the container is always capturing a relatively fresh certificate:

### bounce the reverse proxy every month
39 0 25 * * cd /home/youruser/caddy && /path/to/docker-compose down && /path/to/docker-compose up -d

Items.Title: 弦乐💫 🌌 ✨ | Strings 💫 🌌 ✨
Items.Description:
古尔森🦋

发表于词条：

 The persons name is mark smith and he is 22

词条：

 this string has escape characters this\nstring\nhas\nescape\ncharacters

词条：

符文
符文 bir string'e kodlanmış özel karakterlerdir。

奥内克科德：

 main() { var clapping = '\u{1f30C}'; print(clapping); }

奥内克科德：

 main() { var clapping = '\u{1F31F}'; print(clapping); }

资源：快速学习 Google Flutter - Mark Clow

TheOtherDev/s - TheOtherDev/s - Nov 10 '21 21 年 11 月 10 日

Hosney Ara Smrity - Hosney Ara Smrity - Oct 7 '21 21 年 10 月 7 日

TheOtherDev/s - TheOtherDev/s - Oct 27 '21 21 年 10 月 27 日



原文:

Gülsen 🦋

Posted on Apr 24

${expression} kullanarak bir ifadenin değerini bir string'in içine koyabilirsiniz.

class Person{
String firstName;
String lastName;
int age;
Person(this.firstName, this.lastName, this.age);
}
main(){
Person p = new Person('mark','smith', 22);
print('The persons name is ${p.firstName} ${p.lastName} and he is ${p.age}');
}

Çıktı:

The persons name is mark smith and he is 22

Raw Strings Bir string'i biçimlendirmek için escape karakterinii kullanabilirsiniz. Örneğin: '\n', 'yeni satır' anlamına gelir. Kaçış karakterlerini yok saymak için 'r' kullanın.

main(){
print('this\nstring\nhas\nescape\ncharacters');
print('');
print(r'this\nstring\nhas\nescape\ncharacters');
}

Çıktı:

this
string
has
escape
characters
this\nstring\nhas\nescape\ncharacters

Örnek kod: – ‘Dollar Sign’

void main() {
double price = 100.75;
print('Price is: \$${price}');
}

Çıktı:

Runes
Runes bir string'e kodlanmış özel karakterlerdir.

Örnek kod:

main() {
var clapping = '\u{1f30C}';
print(clapping);
}

Çıktı: 🌌

Örnek kod:

main() {
var clapping = '\u{1F31F}';
print(clapping);
}

Çıktı: 🌟

daha fazlası için: https://www.compart.com/en/unicode/block/U+1F300

Resource: Learn Google Flutter Fast - Mark Clow

TheOtherDev/s - Nov 10 '21

Hosney Ara Smrity - Oct 7 '21

TheOtherDev/s - Oct 27 '21
Items.Author: gulsenkeskin

Items.Title: 如何使用 Blazor WebAssembly 构建 Web 条形码和 QR 码阅读器 | How to Build Web Barcode and QR Code Reader with Blazor WebAssembly
Items.Description:
它使开发人员能够使用 C# 和 HTML 创建 Web 应用程序。 但是，在开发 Blazor 项目时，调用现有的 JavaScript API 是不可避免的。 本文将帮助希望使用 Blazor WebAssembly 和Dynamsoft JavaScript Barcode SDK构建 Web 条形码和 QR 码阅读器应用程序的 Web 开发人员。

如何使用 Dynamsoft JavaScript 条码 SDK

在您的 HTML 文件中包含<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/dynamsoft-javascript-barcode@9.0.0/dist/dbr.js"></script> 。

申请30 天试用许可证以激活 SDK：

 Dynamsoft . DBR . BarcodeReader . license = " DLS2eyJoYW5kc2hha2VDb2RlIjoiMjAwMDAxLTE2NDk4Mjk3OTI2MzUiLCJvcmdhbml6YXRpb25JRCI6IjIwMDAwMSIsInNlc3Npb25QYXNzd29yZCI6IndTcGR6Vm05WDJrcEQ5YUoifQ== " ;

为了方便地处理 JavaScript 互操作调用，我们选择 Blazor WebAssembly 来实现 Web 条形码和 QR 码阅读器。

使用 Blazor WebAssembly 模板创建 Blazor 应用程序：

 dotnet new blazorwasm -o BlazorBarcodeSample

然后在项目中添加一个新页面：

 cd BlazorBarcodeSample
dotnet new razorcomponent -n BarcodeReader -o Pages

之后，将BarcodeReader属性添加到Pages/Index.razor ：

 @page "/" < h1 > Hello , world !</ h1 > Welcome to your new app . < SurveyPrompt Title = "How is Blazor working for you?" /> < BarcodeReader />

至此，我们可以运行应用并查看主页：

第一次运行应用程序时，我们可以打开开发者控制台观察是怎么回事。

进行一些更改并重新构建项目后，我们会发现只重新获取了BlazorBarcodeSample.dll 。

在 Blazor WebAssembly 中读取条形码和 QR 码

我们将Dynamsoft JavaScript Barcode SDK添加到wwwroot/index.html ，然后创建一个jsInterop.js文件用于 JavaScript 和 .NET 之间的互操作：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html> <head> <meta charset= "utf-8" /> <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no" /> <title> BlazorBarcodeSample </title> <base href= "/" /> <link href= "css/bootstrap/bootstrap.min.css" rel= "stylesheet" /> <link href= "css/app.css" rel= "stylesheet" /> <link href= "BlazorBarcodeSample.styles.css" rel= "stylesheet" /> <script src= "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/dynamsoft-javascript-barcode@9.0.0/dist/dbr.js" ></script> </head> <body> <script src= "_framework/blazor.webassembly.js" ></script> <script src= "jsInterop.js" ></script> </body> </html>

在jsInterop.js中，我们初始化 Dynamsoft Barcode Reader 并使用 JavaScript 变量来保存 .NET 对象的引用：

 let barcodescanner = null ; let dotnetRef = null ; window . jsFunctions = { init : function ( obj ) { let result = true ; try { Dynamsoft . DBR . BarcodeReader . license = " DLS2eyJoYW5kc2hha2VDb2RlIjoiMjAwMDAxLTE2NDk4Mjk3OTI2MzUiLCJvcmdhbml6YXRpb25JRCI6IjIwMDAwMSIsInNlc3Npb25QYXNzd29yZCI6IndTcGR6Vm05WDJrcEQ5YUoifQ== " ; barcodescanner = await Dynamsoft . DBR . BarcodeScanner . createInstance (); await barcodescanner . updateRuntimeSettings ( " balance " ); dotnetRef = obj ; } catch ( e ) { console . log ( e ); result = false ; } return result ; }, };

要从磁盘驱动器中选择图像文件，我们使用InputFile 组件：

 < InputFile OnChange = " LoadImage " /> @ code { private async Task LoadImage ( InputFileChangeEventArgs e ) { var imageFile = e . File ; var jsImageStream = imageFile . OpenReadStream ( 1024 * 1024 * 20 ); var dotnetImageStream = new DotNetStreamReference ( jsImageStream ); await JSRuntime . InvokeVoidAsync ( " jsFunctions.setImageUsingStreaming " , " image " , dotnetImageStream ); } }

在加载图像时，我们调用jsInterop.js中的setImageUsingStreaming()方法来传递图像数据：

 function returnResultsAsString ( results ) { let txts = []; try { for ( let i = 0 ; i < results . length ; ++ i ) { txts . push ( results [ i ]. barcodeText ); } let barcoderesults = txts . join ( ' , ' ); if ( txts . length == 0 ) { barcoderesults = ' No barcode found ' ; } if ( dotnetRef ) { dotnetRef . invokeMethodAsync ( ' ReturnBarcodeResultsAsync ' , barcoderesults ); } } catch ( e ) { } } setImageUsingStreaming : async function setImageUsingStreaming ( imageElementId , imageStream ) { const arrayBuffer = await imageStream . arrayBuffer (); const blob = new Blob ([ arrayBuffer ]); const url = URL . createObjectURL ( blob ); document . getElementById ( imageElementId ). src = url ; document . getElementById ( imageElementId ). style . display = ' block ' ; if ( barcodescanner ) { try { let results = await barcodescanner . decode ( blob ); returnResultsAsString ( results ); } catch ( ex ) { alert ( ex . message ); throw ex ; } } },

同时，我们调用decode()方法从图像缓冲区中读取条形码和 QR 码，然后将结果传递给 BarcodeReader.razor 中的BarcodeReader.razor ReturnBarcodeResultsAsync()方法：

 [ JSInvokable ] public void ReturnBarcodeResultsAsync ( String text ) { result = text ; StateHasChanged (); }

现在我们可以使用一些条形码和二维码图像来测试 Blazor WebAssembly 应用程序。

使用相机实时扫描条码和二维码

除了从静态文件中解码条形码和二维码外，我们还可以使用连接的摄像头进行实时扫描。

在BarcodeReader.razor文件中，我们添加了一个实时扫描按钮：

 < button class =" btn btn - primary " @onclick=" LiveScan ">Live Scan</button> @code { public async Task LiveScan () { await JSRuntime . InvokeVoidAsync ( "jsFunctions.liveScan" ); } }

然后在jsInterop.js中添加对应的 JavaScript 函数：

 liveScan : async function () { if ( barcodescanner ) { barcodescanner . onFrameRead = async results => { if ( results . length > 0 ) { console . log ( " onFrameRead " ); returnResultsAsString ( results ); } }; await barcodescanner . show (); } else { alert ( " The barcode reader is still initializing. " ); } },

当我们单击该按钮时，将出现一个包含相机预览和相机源列表的弹出窗口。

这是该项目的最终外观：

源代码

https://github.com/yushulx/blazor-barcode-qrcode-reader-scanner https://github.com/yushulx/blazor-barcode-qrcode-reader-scanner



原文:

Blazor is a web framework developed by Microsoft. It enables developers to create web apps using C# and HTML. However, calling existing JavaScript API is inevitable while developing a Blazor project. This article will help web developers who want to build web barcode and QR code reader apps using Blazor WebAssembly and Dynamsoft JavaScript Barcode SDK.

How to Use Dynamsoft JavaScript Barcode SDK

Include <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/dynamsoft-javascript-barcode@9.0.0/dist/dbr.js"></script> in your HTML file.

Apply for a 30-day trial license to activate the SDK:

Dynamsoft.DBR.BarcodeReader.license = "DLS2eyJoYW5kc2hha2VDb2RlIjoiMjAwMDAxLTE2NDk4Mjk3OTI2MzUiLCJvcmdhbml6YXRpb25JRCI6IjIwMDAwMSIsInNlc3Npb25QYXNzd29yZCI6IndTcGR6Vm05WDJrcEQ5YUoifQ==";

Blazor Architecture Overview

Blazor provides two templates: Blazor WebAssembly and Blazor Server.

Blazor WebAssembly

Blazor Server

Blazor server runs client-side UI logic on the server-side and sends the UI changes via Websocket. To conveniently handle JavaScript interop calls, we pick Blazor WebAssembly to implement the web barcode and QR code reader.

New to Blazor WebAssembly Project

Create a Blazor App with the Blazor WebAssembly template:

dotnet new blazorwasm -o BlazorBarcodeSample

Then add a new page to the project:

cd BlazorBarcodeSample
dotnet new razorcomponent -n BarcodeReader -o Pages

After that, add a BarcodeReader attribute to Pages/Index.razor:

@page "/" <h1>Hello, world!</h1> Welcome to your new app. <SurveyPrompt Title="How is Blazor working for you?" />
<BarcodeReader />

So far, we can run the app and view the homepage:

When running the app for the first time, we can open the developer console to observe what is going on.

A dotnet.wasm file and some *.dll files are fetched and cached by the web browser.

After making some changes and rebuild the project, we will find that only the BlazorBarcodeSample.dll has been fetched again.

Reading Barcode and QR Code in Blazor WebAssembly

We add Dynamsoft JavaScript Barcode SDK to wwwroot/index.html, and then create a jsInterop.js file for interoperation between JavaScript and .NET:

<!DOCTYPE html>
<html> <head> <meta charset="utf-8" /> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, maximum-scale=1.0, user-scalable=no" /> <title>BlazorBarcodeSample</title> <base href="/" /> <link href="css/bootstrap/bootstrap.min.css" rel="stylesheet" /> <link href="css/app.css" rel="stylesheet" /> <link href="BlazorBarcodeSample.styles.css" rel="stylesheet" /> <script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/dynamsoft-javascript-barcode@9.0.0/dist/dbr.js"></script>
</head> <body> <script src="_framework/blazor.webassembly.js"></script> <script src="jsInterop.js"></script>
</body> </html>

In jsInterop.js, we initialize Dynamsoft Barcode Reader and use a JavaScript variable to save the reference of the .NET object:

let barcodescanner = null;
let dotnetRef = null;
window.jsFunctions = { init: function(obj) { let result = true; try { Dynamsoft.DBR.BarcodeReader.license = "DLS2eyJoYW5kc2hha2VDb2RlIjoiMjAwMDAxLTE2NDk4Mjk3OTI2MzUiLCJvcmdhbml6YXRpb25JRCI6IjIwMDAwMSIsInNlc3Npb25QYXNzd29yZCI6IndTcGR6Vm05WDJrcEQ5YUoifQ=="; barcodescanner = await Dynamsoft.DBR.BarcodeScanner.createInstance(); await barcodescanner.updateRuntimeSettings("balance"); dotnetRef = obj; } catch (e) { console.log(e); result = false; } return result; },
};

The JavaScript init() function will be triggered by the OnInitialized() method in BarcodeReader.razor:

@inject IJSRuntime JSRuntime @if (initialized == false)
{ <p>Initializing...</p>
}
else
{
} @code { Boolean initialized = false; protected override void OnInitialized() { Init(); } public async void Init() { initialized = await JSRuntime.InvokeAsync<Boolean>("jsFunctions.init", DotNetObjectReference.Create(this)); StateHasChanged(); } }

To select an image file from the disk drive, we use the InputFile component:

<InputFile OnChange="LoadImage" />
@code { private async Task LoadImage(InputFileChangeEventArgs e) { var imageFile = e.File; var jsImageStream = imageFile.OpenReadStream(1024 * 1024 * 20); var dotnetImageStream = new DotNetStreamReference(jsImageStream); await JSRuntime.InvokeVoidAsync("jsFunctions.setImageUsingStreaming", "image", dotnetImageStream); }
}

As the image is loaded, we call the setImageUsingStreaming() method in jsInterop.js to pass the image data:

function returnResultsAsString(results) { let txts = []; try { for (let i = 0; i < results.length; ++i) { txts.push(results[i].barcodeText); } let barcoderesults = txts.join(', '); if (txts.length == 0) { barcoderesults = 'No barcode found'; } if (dotnetRef) { dotnetRef.invokeMethodAsync('ReturnBarcodeResultsAsync', barcoderesults); } } catch (e) { }
} setImageUsingStreaming: async function setImageUsingStreaming(imageElementId, imageStream) { const arrayBuffer = await imageStream.arrayBuffer(); const blob = new Blob([arrayBuffer]); const url = URL.createObjectURL(blob); document.getElementById(imageElementId).src = url; document.getElementById(imageElementId).style.display = 'block'; if (barcodescanner) { try { let results = await barcodescanner.decode(blob); returnResultsAsString(results); } catch (ex) { alert(ex.message); throw ex; } } },

In the meantime, we invoke the decode() method to read barcode and QR code from the image buffer and then pass the results to the ReturnBarcodeResultsAsync() method in BarcodeReader.razor:

[JSInvokable]
public void ReturnBarcodeResultsAsync(String text)
{ result = text; StateHasChanged();
}

Now we can test the Blazor WebAssembly app with some barcode and QR code images.

Live Scan Barcode and QR Code Using Camera

In addition to decoding barcode and QR code from static files, we can also do real-time scan with a connected camera.

In the BarcodeReader.razor file, we add a live scan button:

<button class="btn btn-primary" @onclick="LiveScan">Live Scan</button>

@code { public async Task LiveScan() { await JSRuntime.InvokeVoidAsync( "jsFunctions.liveScan"); }
}

Then add the corresponding JavaScript function in the jsInterop.js:

liveScan: async function () { if (barcodescanner) { barcodescanner.onFrameRead = async results => { if (results.length > 0) { console.log("onFrameRead"); returnResultsAsString(results); } }; await barcodescanner.show(); } else { alert("The barcode reader is still initializing."); }
},

When we click the button, a popup window that contains a camera preview and camera source list will appear.

Here is the final look of the project:

Source Code

https://github.com/yushulx/blazor-barcode-qrcode-reader-scanner

Items.Title: Saiba um pouco sobre o Projeto RSocial | Saiba um pouco sobre o Projeto RSocial
Items.Description:
没有 fim da 登陆页面，tem uma call to action (chamada para ação) que convidar os interessados a fornecer um email para contato，que usare para montar uma newsletter，onde postarei novidades e atualizações do progresso do projeto。

我喜欢，吃！！！



原文:

Oláááá, venho para falar de um projeto que estou desenvolvendo, o RSocial (Recompensa Social) e qual será meus passos iniciais para validar a ideia desse projeto.

O RSocial visa desenvolver um sistema de recompensas com a intenção de incentivar pessoas a contribuirem em causas sociais.

Assim, esse projeto busca ajudar futuramente dois grupos de pessoas que a primeiro momento não tem uma relação uns com os outros, mas que estão praticamente na mesma situação: Trabalhadores Autônomos e Voluntários em Causas Sociais.

Aqui na minha cidade (Corumbá MS), esses dois grupos possuem um tamanho considerável e lidam com situação de viver e atuar só com o suficiente.

Principalmente os vonluntários em causas sociais, que dedicam seus tempos e até mesmo dinheiro para contribuir e fazer bem ao próximo.

Então, desde que eu entrei para a área de desenvolvimento de software eu venho pensando ideias de como utilizar o que eu sei para contribuir. Também tenho buscado por ideias que já deram certo em outros lugares pelo mundo.

Com base em tudo que vi, vejo que o problema não é simples e vejo que só ter ideias não vai fazer o problema ir embora.

É preciso ter uma boa execução e planejamento.

Então antes de sequer começar a executar e planejar muito a fundo (principalmente escrever linhas e linhas de códigos), preciso validar se a ideia é de fato viavel e se vai atrair adeptos.

Por isso vou seguir a abordagem bem interessante do Lean Startup (por Eric Ries) para o processo de validação e desenvolvimento. O MVP por hora é uma landing page básica feita para chamar atenção e despertar curiosidade. No fim da landing page, tem uma call to action (chamada para ação) que convidar os interessados a fornecer um email para contato, que usarei para montar uma newsletter, onde postarei novidades e atualizações do progresso do projeto.

Estou usando a hubspot para realizar a captura desses emails a porpósito.

Também irei utilizar o montante de emails que a landing page conseguir como um KPI para medir o nível de interesse pela ideia. Eu pensei em ativar analytics nessa landing page para medir a interação, mas cheguei a conclusão que o montante de emails que a landing page conseguir é o suficiente por hora.

Além da landing page eu criei um repositório público no github onde esou elaborando a ideia e alguns OKRs, e Senários Gherkin.

O link para a landing page é https://www.onss.tech/recompensa-social/, e a do repositório no github é https://github.com/otavio99/onss-sr.

Por hora é isso, caso você que está lendo este post nesse momento tenha alguma dúvida ou quer dar sugestões e feedbacks, estou sempre por aqui na dev.to.

Me desejem sorte, até!!!

Items.Title: 深入了解 JavaScript 中的变量 | Digging more into Variables in JavaScript
Items.Description:
艾莉亚克里希纳

发表于现在我们对变量有了基本的了解，我们需要更多地了解这些变量内部的内容，这些变量是不同的 DataType。

我们在应用程序内部工作的一切最终都归结为原始数据类型。

  • 原始数据类型包括未定义、字符串、数字和布尔值

  • 未定义的变量还没有被赋值。如果我们在变量中没有放任何东西，它就是未定义的类型。

  • 字符串是一系列字符，并用引号括起来
var myStringWelcome = "Hello World";
  • 数字可以是整数或小数
var myNumberInt = 100; var myNumberDecimal = 100.100;
  • 布尔值有两个值：真或假
var isMyBooleanTrue = true; var isMyBooleanFalse = false;

迈克尔·恩沃科查 - 拉姆·马赫什瓦里 ⚡ - Apr 23 4月23日



原文:

Arya Krishna

Posted on Apr 25

Now that we have a basic idea about the variable , we need to understand more about what goes inside those variable which are the different DataTypes.

Everything that we work inside of an application ultimately comes down to Primitive DataTypes.

  • Primitive data types include undefined, string, number and boolean

  • Undefined variables haven't been assigned values yet.If we put nothing inside a variable it is type of UnDefined.

  • A string is a series of characters and is surrounded by quotes 
var myStringWelcome = "Hello World";
  • A number can be either an integer or a decimal 
var myNumberInt = 100;
var myNumberDecimal = 100.100;
  • Booleans have two values: true or false 
var isMyBooleanTrue = true;
var isMyBooleanFalse = false;

JavaScript is loosely typed, so the type is tied to the value held in the variable, not the variable itself!

Michael Nwaokocha - Apr 23

Mahmoud EL-kariouny - Apr 23

Ram Maheshwari ⚡ - Apr 23

Items.Title: 使用 GitHub 存储库创建免费静态 API | Creating a Free Static API using a GitHub Repository
Items.Description:
静态 API 只是在线托管的文件（通常是 JSON）的集合，应用程序可以通过 HTTP 请求进行访问。 与动态 API 不同，静态 API 无法执行操作。

为什么使用静态 API

由于动态 API 要求您在服务器上运行应用程序，因此它们可能很昂贵且难以维护（尤其是在规模化时）。 有时您的应用程序需要通过 HTTP 请求访问数据，但不需要 API 可以提供的其他功能。 如果是您，静态 API 可以为您节省时间和金钱，特别是如果您使用免费的静态托管解决方案，例如 GitHub。

何时使用静态 API

在以下情况下考虑使用静态 API：

  • 您不需要对请求进行身份验证
  • 您只需要交付预制的内容
  • 操作不需要由请求触发
  • 内容不经常更新

如何使用 GitHub 创建静态 API

要创建静态 API，您只需在线某处托管 JSON 文件。 以下是使用 GitHub 存储库免费执行此操作的方法：

创建一个 JSON 文件，其中包含您希望每个请求返回的数据。 描述性地命名文件，以便您知道您在代码中请求的内容。

如果您从未使用过 GitHub，则需要创建一个免费帐户。 这是有关如何创建存储库的文档。

这是有关如何上传文件的文档。

最简单的方法是使用 GitHub RAW 文件 URL，但是，这不是最佳实践。 在您的最终应用程序中，强烈建议您使用GitHub Pages ，它将通过 CDN 为您的文件提供更好的性能。 我将解释这两种方法。

方法一：GitHub Pages（推荐）

按照他们的文档了解如何在您的存储库中启用 GitHub Pages 。 不要担心给你的网站一个主题，因为网站的外观并不重要。

在存储库中设置 GitHub Pages 后，使用以下 URL 模式访问 JSON 文件：https://.github.io///

方法 2：GitHub RAW 文件 URL

此方法不需要任何额外的设置。 原始文件的 URL 如下所示：
示例： https ://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/platform-samples/master/LICENSE.txt

在上面的示例中，用户“github”有一个名为“platform-samples”的存储库，其中一个名为“master”的分支包含根目录中的文件“LICENSE.txt”。

如果文件位于存储库的文件夹中，而不是根目录中，请记住在 URL 中包含路径。 示例： https ://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/platform-samples/master/sql/metrics/actions-summary.sql

补充笔记

您可以使用 GitHub 原始文件链接从任何公共 GitHub 存储库访问文件，而不仅仅是您自己的。 如果其他人将数据提供给公众使用，如果他们没有通过 API 提供数据（或者如果他们的 API 不适合您的需求），这可能是访问数据的好方法。

但是，存储库的所有者可以随时修改或删除他们的文件。 为避免更改破坏您的应用程序，请分叉存储库并从您的分叉中获取数据。



原文:

Most APIs on the web are dynamic, meaning they're capable of generating, updating, and transforming content in response to requests. A static API is simply a collection of files (typically JSON) that are hosted online and can be accessed via HTTP request by an application. Unlike a dynamic API, a static API is unable to perform actions.

Why use a Static API

Because dynamic APIs require you to run an application on a server, they can be expensive and difficult to maintain (especially at scale). Sometimes your application needs to access data via HTTP requests but doesn't need the other functionality an API can provide. If that's you, a static API can save you time and money, especially if you make use of a free static hosting solution, like GitHub.

When to use a Static API

Consider using a static API if:

  • You don't need to authenticate requests
  • You only need to deliver pre-made content
  • Actions don't need to be triggered by requests
  • The content is not updated frequently

How to use GitHub to Create Your Static API

To create a static API, all you need is to host JSON files somewhere online. Here's how you can do so for free using a GitHub repository:

1. Create your JSON files.

Create a JSON file containing the data you want each request to return. Name the files descriptively so you know what you're requesting in your code.

2. Create a public GitHub repository.

If you've never used GitHub, you'll need to create a free account. Here's the documentation on how to create a repository.

3. Upload your JSON files to the repository.

Here's documentation on how to upload files.

4. Access your files via URL.

The easiest way to do this is using GitHub RAW file URLs, HOWEVER, this is not best practice. In your final application, it's highly recommended that you use GitHub Pages, which will serve your files via CDN for better performance. I'll explain both methods.

Method 1: GitHub Pages (Recommended)

GitHub Pages is GitHub's free static hosting solution. Follow their documentation on how to enable GitHub Pages in your repository. Don't worry about giving your website a theme as the appearance of the site won't matter.

Once you've set up GitHub Pages in your repository, access your JSON files using this URL pattern: https://.github.io///

Method 2: GitHub RAW File URLs

This method doesn't require any additional setup. The URL to your raw file will look like this:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/////

Example: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/platform-samples/master/LICENSE.txt

In the above example, the user "github" has a repository named "platform-samples" with a branch named "master" that contains the file "LICENSE.txt" in the root directory.

If the file is in a folder in the repository, rather than the root directory, remember to include the path in the URL. Example: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/github/platform-samples/master/sql/metrics/actions-summary.sql

Additional Notes

You can use GitHub raw file links to access files from any public GitHub repository, not just your own. If someone else has made data available for public use, this can be a great way to access it if they haven't made it available via an API (or if their API doesn't suit your needs).

HOWEVER The owner of the repository could modify or remove their files at any point. To avoid changes breaking your application, fork the repository and fetch the data from your fork instead.
Items.Author: darrian

Items.Title: JS 的 Rest 和 Spread 运算符 | The Rest and the Spread operator of JS
Items.Description:
发表于大家好！ 我希望你们都做得很好。 曾经听过开发人员谈论“传播”运算符，或者只是在 JS 代码中看到“...”[三点]，并想知道这是什么东西。

好吧，我们今天将介绍这一点。

让我们一个一个看一下。

休息运算符

例子：

 function foo (... bar ){ return bar ; } console . log ( foo ( 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 )); // [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ] console . log ( foo ( 1 , 2 )); // [ 1, 2 ]

无论您传递给函数的参数数量是多少，rest 运算符都会收集所有参数并将它们“压缩”成一个数组，这在很多情况下都很有用。

它也可以这样使用：

 function foo ( a , b ,... bar ){ console . log ( a ); // 1 console . log ( b ); // 2 console . log ( bar ); // [ 3, 4 ] } foo ( 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 );

我们所做的是告诉函数我们将传递“a”、“b”，然后我们传递的任何内容都将其压缩成一个数组。 现在，您可能会想到一些极端情况，例如“如果我……会发生什么”。 别担心，我也正在这样做。

如果我没有为其余运算符传递值会发生什么？


 function foo ( a , b ,... bar ){ console . log ( a ); // 1 console . log ( b ); // 2 console . log ( bar ); // [] } foo ( 1 , 2 );

在这里，rest 参数没有得到任何值，这会导致一个空数组。

但是，如果我将 rest 参数移到第一个或第二个位置会发生什么？

 function zen ( a ,... bar , b ){ // SyntaxError: Rest parameter must be last formal parameter console . log ( a ); console . log ( b ); console . log ( bar ); } zen ( 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 );

这不会运行。 错误中清楚地提到了原因。
其余参数必须是最后一个函数参数。

现在我们知道了，什么是休息运算符。 让我们看一个用例。

假设有人说您创建了一个函数，该函数接受两个值并返回它们的总和。 简单的？ 不是吗。 让我们升级一下，现在您需要创建相同的函数，但可能有任意数量的参数，并且您的函数必须返回所有参数的总和。

一种方法是为不同数量的参数创建不同的函数。 既然您知道了其余运算符，您就不必担心这一点。

 function addValues (... values ){ let sum = 0 ; for ( let i = 0 ; i < values . length ; i ++ ){ sum += values [ i ] } return sum ; } console . log ( addValues ( 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 , 5 )) // 15

注意：我知道我可以使用 foreach 甚至 reduce 方法来完成相同的任务，但为了让这篇文章对所有人都通用，我使用了 for 循环。好吧，改天再做一个话题。

扩展运算符

早些时候，我告诉过你，rest 和 spread 具有相同的语法，但它们仍然是彼此相反的。 在 rest 运算符采用多个值并对其进行压缩的情况下，展开运算符会扩展对象值。

 const arr = [ 1 , 2 , 3 , 4 ]; console . log ( arr ); // [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ] console . log (... arr ); // 1 2 3 4

正如您所看到的，扩展运算符扩展了数组的值，它也适用于对象。

扩展运算符可用于许多不同的任务，例如：

  • 复制数组
  • 在 React 中添加状态
  • 连接或组合数组
  • 使用数学函数
  • 将 NodeList 转换为数组
  • 使用数组作为参数
  • 将项目添加到列表
  • 组合对象

传播也适用于对象。

 const obj = { " hello " : " world " , " foo " : " bar " } console . log ( obj ) // { hello: 'world', foo: 'bar' } console . log (... obj ) // TypeError: Found non-callable @@iterator console . log ({... obj }) // { hello: 'world', foo: 'bar' }

您可以创建对象的克隆并在其键的帮助下更新其值。

 // Creating array const arr = [ 1 , 2 , 3 ]; console . log ([... arr , 4 , 5 ]); // [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ] // Creating Object const obj = { " hello " : " world " , " foo " : " bar " }; console . log ({... obj , " foo " : " zen " }); // { hello: 'world', foo: 'zen' }

最后的想法

自发布以来，其余参数和扩展运算符已成为任何 Web 项目不可或缺的一部分。 我希望我对你说清楚了。
谢谢阅读。

好的，这就是 Umang Mittal 的签名。



原文:

umangm05

Posted on Apr 25

Hello folks! I hope you are all doing great. Ever heard developers talking about "spread" operators or just saw "..." [Triple Dots] in JS code and wondered what is this thing.

Well, we're going to cover that today.

The syntax of rest and spread operator looks exactly the same but they are polar opposite of each other in terms of functionality.

Let's look at both of them one by one.

The Rest operator

The rest operator is used to take an indefinite number of arguments in a function and condenses it to an array. Example:

function foo(...bar){ return bar;
} console.log(foo(1,2,3,4)); // [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
console.log(foo(1,2)); // [ 1, 2 ]

Whatever the number of parameters you pass into a function the rest operator collects all the arguments and "condenses" them into an Array which can be useful in a lot of cases.

It can also be used like this:

function foo(a,b,...bar){ console.log(a); // 1 console.log(b); // 2 console.log(bar); // [ 3, 4 ]
} foo(1,2,3,4);

What we've done is told the function that we are going to pass "a", "b" and whatever we pass after that, condense it into an array. Now, you might me thinking of some edge cases like "what will happen if I ... ". Don't worry I am getting on that too.

What happens if I pass no value for the rest oprator?


function foo(a,b,...bar){ console.log(a); // 1 console.log(b); // 2 console.log(bar); // []
} foo(1,2);

Here, the rest parameter does not gets any value which results in an empty array.

But, what happens if I shift the rest parameter to the first or the second position?

function zen(a,...bar,b){ // SyntaxError: Rest parameter must be last formal parameter console.log(a); console.log(b); console.log(bar);
} zen(1,2,3,4);

This will not run. The reason is clearly mentioned in the error.
The rest parameter must be the last function argument.

Now that we know, what is rest operator. Let's look at a use-case.

Suppose, you were said to create a function that takes two values and returns the sum of them. Easy? isn't it. Let's escalate it, Now you need to create the same function but there could be any possible number of parameters and your function must return the sum of all of the arguments.

One approach is that you create different functions for different number of parameters. Now that you know the rest operator, you must'nt worry about that.

function addValues(...values){ let sum = 0; for(let i = 0; i< values.length;i++){ sum += values[i] } return sum;
} console.log(addValues(1,2,3,4,5)) // 15

Note: I know I could've used foreach or even reduce method to do the same task but to keep this post general for all the people, I used the for loop.Well, another topic for another day.

The Spread operator

Earlier, I told you that both rest and spread has same syntax and still they are opposites of each other. Where rest operator takes multiple values and condenses them , the spread opeartor expands the object values.

const arr = [1,2,3,4];
console.log(arr); // [ 1, 2, 3, 4 ]
console.log(...arr); // 1 2 3 4

As you can see the spread operator spreads the value of the array and it also works on the object.

Spread Operator can be used in many different tasks like:

  • Copying an array
  • Adding to state in React
  • Concatenating or combining arrays
  • Using Math functions
  • Converting NodeList to an array
  • Using an array as arguments
  • Adding an item to a list
  • Combining objects

The spread works on the objects too.

const obj = { "hello" : "world", "foo" : "bar"
} console.log(obj) // { hello: 'world', foo: 'bar' }
console.log(...obj) // TypeError: Found non-callable @@iterator
console.log({...obj}) // { hello: 'world', foo: 'bar' }

You can create a clone of an object and updates it's value with the help of it's key.

// Creating array
const arr = [1,2,3];
console.log([...arr, 4,5]); // [ 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 ] // Creating Object
const obj = { "hello" : "world", "foo" : "bar"
}; console.log({...obj, "foo" : "zen"}); // { hello: 'world', foo: 'zen' }

Final Thoughts

The rest parameters and the spread operator has become an integral part of any web project since it's launch. I hope I made both of them clear to you.
Thanks for reading.

Okay, so that's Umang Mittal signing off.
Items.Author: umangm05

Items.Title: 如何在 Socket IO 中发送和接收数据？ | How to send and receive data in Socket IO?
Items.Description:
苏珊塔·古普塔

发表于根据 socket io 的官网，Socket.IO 是一个库，可以实现客户端和服务器之间的低延迟、双向和基于事件的通信。

基于事件是什么意思？ 我们可以使用下面的代码块在 Socket 中发送和接收数据。 我们可以从服务器发送一个事件并在客户端接收它，反之亦然。

接收数据socket.on("message", (hello) => { console.log(hello); // world

发送数据
一般来说，发送和接收数据的代码是接收数据：socket.on(eventName, listenerFn)

发送数据：socket.emit(eventName, Data)

我们必须使用相同的 eventName 来发送和捕获数据。 帕特里克·蒙泰罗 - 库尔迪普·塔拉帕拉 - Apr 14 4月14日



原文:

Sushanta Gupta

Posted on Apr 25

Socket.io is a library to abstract the WebSocket connections. According to the official website of socket io, Socket.IO is a library that enables low-latency, bidirectional and event-based communication between a client and a server.

What does it mean by event-based? We can send and receive data in Socket by using the following code block. We can send an event from the server and receive it in the client and vice-versa.

To Receive Data socket.on("message", (hello) => { console.log(hello); // world

});

To send Data
socket.emit("message", "hello");

In general, the code for sending and receiving data is Receiving Data: socket.on(eventName, listenerFn)

Sending Data : socket.emit(eventName, Data)

We have to use same eventName for sending and catching data. There are some pre-defined events in Socket Io but we can also make our own custom events provided that the same event name is to be used in both client and server.

Patrick Monteiro - Apr 19

Kuldeep Tarapara - Apr 14
Items.Author: sushantagupta007

Items.Title: [已解决] MSBUILD：错误 MSB3428：无法加载 Visual C++ 组件“VCBuild.exe” | [Solved] MSBUILD : error MSB3428: Could not load the Visual C++ component "VCBuild.exe"
Items.Description:
尝试在 React 应用程序中运行npm install时遇到以下错误：

 npm ERR! MSBUILD : error MSB3428: Could not load the Visual C++ component "VCBuild.exe". To fix this, 1) install the .NET Framework 2.0 SDK, 2) install Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 or 3) add the location of the component to the system path if it is installed elsewhere. [..\node_modules\node-sass\build\binding.sln]

解决方案：

尝试在管理员 Windows PowerShell 实例上运行以下命令的众所周知的解决方案后，此错误未能得到解决：

 npm install -g node-gyp npm install --global --production windows-build-tools

图 1：运行上述命令后 PowerShell 冻结

由于运行上述命令后 PowerShell 冻结，我完全放弃了在 Windows 操作系统上运行npm install的尝试。

相反，我将应用程序转移到了一个 Ubuntu VM 实例，并且能够毫无问题地运行npm install 。
所以尝试创建一个 Linux VM 并将你的代码库移植到它上面。 然后运行npm install看看它是否有效。

虽然这种方法不能直接解决 Windows 上的错误，但如果您不介意在 Linux 环境中工作，我相信它是最快、最实用的解决方法。

结论

感谢您阅读这篇博文！

如果您有任何问题或疑虑，请随时在此帖子中发表评论，我会在找到时间时回复您。

如果您觉得这篇文章有帮助，请分享并确保在Twitter和GitHub 上关注我，在LinkedIn上与我联系并订阅我的YouTube 频道。



原文:

Running into the following error when trying to run npm install in a React application:

npm ERR! MSBUILD : error MSB3428: Could not load the Visual C++ component "VCBuild.exe". 
To fix this, 
1) install the .NET Framework 2.0 SDK, 
2) install Microsoft Visual Studio 2005 or 3) 
add the location of the component to the system path if it is installed elsewhere.  
[..\node_modules\node-sass\build\binding.sln]

Solution:

This error has failed to be resolved after trying the well known solution of running the following commands on an Administrator Windows PowerShell instance:

npm install -g node-gyp
npm install --global --production windows-build-tools

Figure 1: PowerShell freezes after running the above commands

Due to PowerShell freezing after running the above commands, I abandoned trying to run npm install on a Windows OS altogether.

Instead, I transferred over the application to a Ubuntu VM instance and was able to run npm install with no issues.
So try creating a Linux VM and porting over your codebase onto it. Then run npm install and see if it works.

While this method does not directly solve the error on Windows, I believe it is the quickest and most pragmatic workaround if you don't mind working in a Linux environment.

Conclusion

Thanks for reading this blog post!

If you have any questions or concerns please feel free to post a comment in this post and I will get back to you when I find the time.

If you found this article helpful please share it and make sure to follow me on Twitter and GitHub, connect with me on LinkedIn and subscribe to my YouTube channel.
Items.Author: shehanat

Items.Title: 通过 AWS Lambda + API Gateway 上的 Puppeteer 从 HTML 生成 PDF | Generate PDFs from HTML via Puppeteer on AWS Lambda + API Gateway
Items.Description:
前言

拥有使您能够使用 HTML && CSS 功能生成 PDF 文件而无需依赖依赖于一大堆 C库的过于复杂的驱动程序的功能会很棒吗？

同时还支持HTML5 && CSS3 的所有最新功能？

好吧，我们有好消息。 有一个名为Puppeteer的框架，它使用相对较新的 Chrome功能，并可以通过基于 NodeJS 的 API 访问它。

虽然我们可以创建一个正在运行的Docker Puppeteer 实例并将其部署在 ECS 或 Heroku 上。 创建稳定和优化的图像可能非常具有挑战性......

相比之下，在 AWS Lambda IHMO中运行实例在开发速度、调试和监控方面会简单得多。 此外，无服务器是 POC 的一个很好的概念（你为你使用的东西付费）

回购 -> 最终结果

你可以在这个repo中看到完整的工作示例

通过在 AWS Lambda 上运行的 Puppeteer 生成 PDF 文档

此 repo 包含一个无服务器应用程序，该应用程序采用 HTML 模板并以二进制形式返回 PDF

图表

要求

  • 码头工人
  • 克隆这个 repo git clone https://github.com/zahaar/generate-pdf-lambda

  • 将cUrl导入Insomnia （不推荐使用 Postman，因为它无法可视化 Pdf）。

  • 运行make api-local以运行本地 API GW。

  • 通过 Insomnia 发送cUrl请求。

    • 您还可以通过在file中提供示例事件并运行make invokation-local来调用 Lambda 绕过 API GW。 响应将是 base64 编码的 PDF 二进制文件。

    必须配置 AWS CLI V2 -> AWS 控制台帐户 && API 密钥

    1. make deploy

    2. 获取 AWS SAM 部署输出 URL Value ，并将 Insomnia 中的 Url 从localhost更改为该值执行结果...

要求和先决条件

  • 码头工人
  • 在此步骤中，本地 SAM 执行设置将完成。 一旦完成，我们将有一个强有力的参考点。

    此步骤的最终版本可以从1_local-setup分支获取

    我们可以通过运行sam init或参考指南来创建基本的 SAM 模板

    但我们的最终目标应该是像这样的复杂结构

    ├── Makefile ├── VERSION -- for VERSION tracking, helpful for CI ├── envs.json -- to sep envs for local execution ( if necessary ) ├── events │  └── api-gw-event.json -- an example API GW event for local execution ├── src │  └── app.js -- main source code file └── template.yaml -- AWS SAM configuration template

    而template.yml有一个 API GW Service 的资源配置

    ... ... ApiGatewayApi: Type: AWS::Serverless::Api Properties: StageName: Staging BinaryMediaTypes: - application~1pdf // Note the support for binary pdf media Type ...

    和 Lambda。 根据我们目标的上下文，它被称为PdfFunction
    记下此配置中使用的Layer 。 通过设置chrome-aws-lambda ，我们基本上排除了在 AWS 在 EC2 上为 Lambdas 使用的Docker映像上为puppeteer和chrome设置package.json依赖项的需要，因为这一步可能非常具有挑战性。

     ... ... PdfFunction: Type: AWS::Serverless::Function Description: Invoked by EventBridge scheduled rule Properties: CodeUri: src/ Handler: app.handler Runtime: nodejs12.x Timeout: 15 MemorySize: 3008 Layers: - !Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:764866452798:layer:chrome-aws-lambda:22' Environment: Variables: EXAMPLE_ENV: 'CHANGE_THIS' Events: ApiEvent: Type: Api Properties: Path: /pdf Method: post RestApiId: Ref: ApiGatewayApi ...

    为了测试是否满足所有要求，让我们运行一个本地事件。

    输出应该与 CloudWatch 中的执行日志基本相同

    ... ... Mounting /Users/wparker/Dev/scheduled-website-screenshot-app/.aws-sam/build/PdfFunction as /var/task:ro,delegated inside runtime container START RequestId: e4d7743d-5be2-4735-84c8-9d5160d9a750 Version: $LATEST ...

    供应模板 HTML

    下一步是对app.js进行编程以启动puppeteer ，使用API GW事件中的HTML并返回一个base64编码的响应，该响应将在API GW的Response上decoded 。

    此步骤的最终版本可以从2_generate-pdf分支中获取

    我们需要将 Lambda 处理程序代码更改为类似的内容。 关键要点是：

    1. 此示例中的浏览器启动 args 参数是专门为 AWS Lambda 兼容性而设置的。
     ... ... browser = await chromium . puppeteer . launch ({ args : chromium . args , defaultViewport : chromium . defaultViewport , executablePath : await chromium . executablePath , headless : chromium . headless , ignoreHTTPSErrors : true , })
    1. 返回格式目标设置为模仿 A4 文档。
     ... ... await page . setViewport ({ width : 1080 , height : 1600 , deviceScaleFactor : 1 , isLandscape : true , }) pdf = await page . pdf ({ format : ' a4 ' , margin : { top : ' 0px ' , right : ' 0px ' , bottom : ' 0px ' , left : ' 0px ' , }, }) ...
    1. 响应标头是为pdf文件传输设置的。 要测试此代码，需要一个HTML模板。 我们将使用这个开源的进行演示。
      文档作为正文发送，带有'Content-Type: text/html'

      请注意'Accept: application/pdf' ，这很重要。

      为了测试我们的结果，让我们以本地start-api模式启动本地 SAM。 将cUrl导入Insomnia 。

      结果

      参考main分支上的README.md中的步骤

      尖端

      而不是玩Postman PDF Visualization的把戏。 我强烈建议切换到失眠

      失眠可视化反应邮递员可视化响应
      在这里工作正常。 您不需要公共 ECR 的凭据（您可以在特定情况下使用身份验证），但如果您只想使用它，请删除现有的凭据

      docker logout public.ecr.aws

      然后再次尝试构建。

      也就是说，如果您仍然想使用...

因为这会导致巨大的混乱。

原文:

“Evil cannot create anything new, they can only corrupt and ruin what good forces have invented or made.” - JRR Tolkien.

Preface

Would it be great to have the functionality that would enable you to generate PDF files using HTML && CSS capabilities without the need to rely on overly complex drivers that are dependent on a whole bunch of C libraries?

While also supporting all the latest features of HTML5 && CSS3?

Well, we have great news. There is a framework called Puppeteer that uses relatively new Chrome feature and makes it accessible though a NodeJS based API.

Essentially what Puppeteer does, is: Launches a Chromium browser instance in a headless mode ( not actually opening it ), and allows us to manipulate the browser via set of API command to parse website, retrieve images and generate PDF as if you were actually opening an HTML file in the latest browser version, etc..

While we can create a running Docker Puppeteer instance and deploy that on ECS or Heroku. The creation of stable && optimized image can be quite challenging...

Having a running instance in AWS Lambda IHMO in contrast would be much simpler in terms of development speed, debug and monitoring. Besides, serverless, is a nice concept for POC ( you pay for what you use )

Repo -> End Result

You can see the complete working example in this repo

Generate PDF document via Puppeteer running on AWS Lambda

This repo contains a serverless application that takes a HTML template and return a PDF in form of a binary

Diagram

Requirements

  • Docker
  • AWS SAM
  • AWS Console Account && API Keys

  1. Clone this repo git clone https://github.com/zahaar/generate-pdf-lambda

  2. Import cUrl to Insomnia ( Postman is not recommended, as it can't visualize Pdf ).

  3. Run make api-local to have local API GW running.

  4. Send cUrl request via Insomnia.

You can also invoke Lambda bypassing API GW, by supplying an example event in file, and running make invokation-local. The response would be a base64 encoded PDF binary.

A configured AWS CLI V2 is a must -> AWS Console Account && API Keys

  1. make deploy

  2. Fetch AWS SAM deploy output URL Value, and change the Url in Insomnia from localhost to that value execution result in…

Requirements and Prerequisites

  • AWS SAM
  • Docker
  • AWS Console Account && API Keys

1. SetUp local AWS SAM Template with Chrome Lambda Layer

In this step the local SAM execution setUp will be complete. Once this is done, we will have a strong reference point.

The end version of this step can be fetched from 1_local-setup branch

We can create a basic SAM template by running sam init or reference a guide

but our end goal should be a sophisticated structure like this

├── Makefile
├── VERSION -- for VERSION tracking, helpful for CI
├── envs.json -- to sep envs for local execution ( if necessary )
├── events
│   └── api-gw-event.json -- an example API GW event for local execution
├── src
│   └── app.js -- main source code file
└── template.yaml -- AWS SAM configuration template

app.js contains simple code that will return the same event.body that it receives from example event.

...
...
var response = { statusCode: 200, body: event.body, } return response
...

while template.yml has a resource configuration for API GW Service

...
...
  ApiGatewayApi:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Api
    Properties:
      StageName: Staging
    BinaryMediaTypes:
      - application~1pdf  // Note the support for binary pdf media Type
...

and the Lambda. As per context of our goal, it's called PdfFunction
Take note of the Layer being used in this config. By setting chrome-aws-lambda, we have essentially ruled out the need to set package.json dependencies for puppeteer and chrome on Docker image thar AWS is using on EC2 for Lambdas, as this step can be quite challenging.

...
...
  PdfFunction:
    Type: AWS::Serverless::Function
    Description: Invoked by EventBridge scheduled rule
    Properties:
      CodeUri: src/
      Handler: app.handler
      Runtime: nodejs12.x
      Timeout: 15
      MemorySize: 3008

      Layers:
        - !Sub 'arn:aws:lambda:${AWS::Region}:764866452798:layer:chrome-aws-lambda:22'
      Environment:
        Variables:
          EXAMPLE_ENV: 'CHANGE_THIS'
      Events:
        ApiEvent:
          Type: Api
          Properties:
            Path: /pdf
            Method: post
            RestApiId:
              Ref: ApiGatewayApi
...

To test that all requirements are met, let's run a local event.

The output should be essentially the same as the execution logs in CloudWatch

...
...
Mounting /Users/wparker/Dev/scheduled-website-screenshot-app/.aws-sam/build/PdfFunction as /var/task:ro,delegated inside runtime container
START RequestId: e4d7743d-5be2-4735-84c8-9d5160d9a750 Version: $LATEST
...

2. Configure Puppeteer in Lambda; Supply Template HTML

Next step is to program app.js to start puppeteer, consume HTML from an API GW event and return a base64 encoded response that would be decoded on Response by API GW.

The end version of this step can be fetched from 2_generate-pdf branch

We need to change the Lambda handler code to something like this. File ( File is too long to displayed here )

Key takeaways are:

  1. Browser launch args parameters in this example are set specifically for AWS Lambda compatibility. 
...
... browser = await chromium.puppeteer.launch({ args: chromium.args, defaultViewport: chromium.defaultViewport, executablePath: await chromium.executablePath, headless: chromium.headless, ignoreHTTPSErrors: true, })
  1. The return format goal was set to mimic A4 document. 
...
... await page.setViewport({ width: 1080, height: 1600, deviceScaleFactor: 1, isLandscape: true, }) pdf = await page.pdf({ format: 'a4', margin: { top: '0px', right: '0px', bottom: '0px', left: '0px', }, })
...
  1. The response headers are set for pdf file transfer. isBase64Encoded flag is set to true to inform API GW that it needs to decode the file. 
...
... var response = { statusCode: 200, headers: { 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*', 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET, POST', 'Content-type': 'application/pdf', 'Content-Disposition': 'attachment; filename="foo.pdf"', }, isBase64Encoded: true, body: pdf.toString('base64'), }
...

To test this code, an HTML template is needed. We will use this open-source one for demonstration.
The document is being sent as body with 'Content-Type: text/html'

Please note 'Accept: application/pdf', this is important.

The end result of cUrl request is in this file

To test our result let's start local SAM in local start-api mode. ( akin to a server, contrary to one time invokation)

Import cUrl into Insomnia.

Result

3. Deploy Lambda + API GW via SAM

Refers to step in README.md on main branch

Tips

Instead of playing tricks with Postman PDF Visualization. I highly recommend switching to Insomnia

Insomnia Visualized Response Postman Visualization Response
2. Error: Error building docker image: pull access denied for

Works fine here. You shouldn't need credentials for Public ECR (you can use auth for specific cases) but if you just want to consume it, remove the existing credentials

docker logout public.ecr.aws

and then try the build again.

That said, if you still want to make use of the…

.

3. Consider using latest AWS Lambda feature: Lambda URL
4. Be mindful of HTTP Headers, when dealing with API GW. As this can lead to a huge confusion.

Items.Title: 乙烯基17 | Vinyl17
Items.Description:
我受到启发创建了一个应用程序，允许用户跟踪他们的黑胶唱片收藏。 用户可以通过提供艺术家姓名、专辑名称、年份和流派来将新的黑胶唱片添加到他们的收藏中。 用户可以按流派浏览收藏。 用户还可以从他们的收藏中删除黑胶唱片。 该应用程序还包含有关黑胶唱片的资源列表。

以下是我在将这个项目变为现实时所做的一些笔记。

我使用以下命令创建了一个带有 API 的新 Rails 应用程序：

 rails new PROJECT_backend --api

我只为后端创建了一个 github 存储库，并添加了模型、种子数据、运行迁移、创建控制器、取消注释 CORS，最后添加了快速 jsonapi gem。

我仔细检查了我的路线，并通过使用以下命令创建一个新的 React 应用程序来转移到应用程序的前端：

 npx create-react-app PROJECT

我使用以下命令安装 react-redux。

 npm install react-redux npm intall redux npm install redux-thunk

安装反应路由器 v5

npm install react-router-dom@5

npm start

启动开发服务器



原文:

I was inspired to create an application that would allow a user to keep track of their vinyl record collection. The user is able to add a new vinyl to their collection by providing an artist name, album name, year, and genre. The user can browse the collection by genre. A user can also delete a vinyl record from their collection. The application also has a list of resources about vinyl records.

Here are a few notes that I took while bringing this project to life.

I created a new rails application with an API using the following command:

rails new PROJECT_backend --api

I created a github repository for only the backend and added models, seed data, ran migrations, created the controllers, uncommented CORS, and lastly added the fast jsonapi gem.

I double checked my routes and moved on to the frontend of the application by creating a new React application using the following command:

npx create-react-app PROJECT

I used the following commands to install react-redux.

npm install react-redux
npm intall redux
npm install redux-thunk

Installed React Router v5

npm install react-router-dom@5

npm start

starts the development server
